probe_designs.probe_name: Per-Attribute Label Prevalence and Class Imbalance Audit
probe_designs.content: Compute the positive-class prevalence p_k = mean(y_k) across the train/val/test splits for each of the 40 CelebA attributes. CelebA is known to have extreme per-attribute imbalance (e.g., 'Bald' ≈ 2%, 'Mustache' ≈ 4%, vs. 'No_Beard' ≈ 83%, 'Young' ≈ 77%). Visualize as a bar chart and verify consistency across splits (max |p_train - p_test| should be small, < 0.02). Healthy signal: prevalence distributions match across splits, and the model's predicted positive rate matches ground-truth prevalence (no prevalence collapse). Unhealthy signal: the model predicts the majority class constantly for rare attributes (e.g., all-negative predictions for 'Bald'), yielding high accuracy but zero recall. Pair this audit with class-weighted BCE or focal loss ablations to check if imbalance is the driver of poor minority recall.
possible_sources: Liu et al., 'Deep Learning Face Attributes in the Wild' (ICCV 2015) — original CelebA paper with attribute statistics
Rudd et al., 'MOON: A Mixed Objective Optimization Network for the Recognition of Facial Attributes' (ECCV 2016)
Lin et al., 'Focal Loss for Dense Object Detection' (ICCV 2017)

probe_designs.probe_name: Per-Attribute BCE Loss Decomposition and Training Curves
probe_designs.content: Decompose the aggregate BCEWithLogitsLoss into 40 per-attribute loss curves logged across training steps/epochs on both train and val. Inspect convergence behavior of each attribute independently. Healthy signal: monotonic decrease on train for all 40 attributes, val loss decreasing then plateauing with a small generalization gap; no attribute's val loss diverges while train loss decreases. Unhealthy signal: a subset of attributes (often rare or visually ambiguous such as 'Pale_Skin', 'Oval_Face', 'Big_Lips') shows val loss increasing or oscillating → localized overfitting; or loss stuck at log(2) ≈ 0.693 indicating the logit collapses to zero. Also track the ratio of positive-term vs. negative-term contributions in BCE to detect imbalance-driven gradient domination.
possible_sources: Goodfellow, Bengio, Courville 'Deep Learning' textbook — training dynamics chapter
Rudd et al., 'MOON' (ECCV 2016) — per-attribute error reporting convention for CelebA
Hand & Chellappa, 'Attributes for Improved Attributes' (AAAI 2017)

probe_designs.probe_name: Per-Attribute Accuracy, F1, and mAP vs. CelebA Benchmarks
probe_designs.content: Report the 40-way per-attribute metrics on the CelebA test split: binary accuracy (standard CelebA reporting), balanced accuracy, macro-F1, per-attribute mean AP, and the mean across attributes. Compare to published baselines: MOON (~90.94% mean accuracy), LNets+ANet (87%), ResNet-50 fine-tuned (~91-92%). Healthy signal: mean accuracy ≥ 90%, and no attribute accuracy drops below 70%; mAP comparable to or exceeding published timm backbones on CelebA. Unhealthy signal: mean accuracy inflated by trivial majority-class prediction on rare attributes — flagged when balanced accuracy is 10+ points below raw accuracy, or when per-attribute F1 for rare classes (Bald, Mustache, Gray_Hair) is < 0.3.
possible_sources: Liu et al., ICCV 2015 CelebA paper
Rudd et al., 'MOON' (ECCV 2016)
He et al., 'MOON/ResNet baselines on CelebA'
Papers With Code CelebA attribute classification leaderboard

probe_designs.probe_name: Sigmoid Probability Calibration (ECE and Reliability Diagrams)
probe_designs.content: For each of the 40 attribute heads, compute Expected Calibration Error (ECE) and Maximum Calibration Error (MCE) using 15 equal-width bins of sigmoid(z). Plot per-attribute reliability diagrams (confidence vs. empirical positive rate). Compute Brier score per attribute. Healthy signal: reliability curves close to the y=x diagonal, ECE < 0.05 per attribute, Brier score comparable to or lower than well-tuned baselines. Unhealthy signal: systematic over-confidence (curve below diagonal in high-confidence bins) — a well-documented pathology of deep classifiers trained with BCE. Apply temperature scaling per-attribute as a post-hoc fix and report pre/post ECE to quantify miscalibration severity.
possible_sources: Guo et al., 'On Calibration of Modern Neural Networks' (ICML 2017)
Naeini et al., 'Obtaining Well Calibrated Probabilities Using Bayesian Binning' (AAAI 2015)
Kumar et al., 'Verified Uncertainty Calibration' (NeurIPS 2019)

probe_designs.probe_name: Demographic Subgroup Disparity on Male/Young/Pale_Skin Slices
probe_designs.content: Using Male, Young, and Pale_Skin attributes as proxy sensitive attributes, evaluate each of the remaining 37 attribute predictions separately on each subgroup. Compute per-attribute TPR, FPR, accuracy disparity (|Acc_group_A - Acc_group_B|), and equalized-odds gap. Healthy signal: disparities < 5% across subgroups for most attributes; any larger gap is explainable by genuine base-rate differences (e.g., 'Mustache' on Male vs. Female is inherently skewed). Unhealthy signal: large gaps (>10%) on non-gendered attributes like 'Smiling', 'Wearing_Hat', 'Eyeglasses' across Male/Female, indicating spurious correlation with demographics. CelebA is documented as biased (Pale_Skin, celebrity-centric) — known-failure mode is makeup/hair attributes binding to gender.
possible_sources: Buolamwini & Gebru, 'Gender Shades' (FAccT 2018)
Ramaswamy et al., 'Fair Attribute Classification through Latent Space De-biasing' (CVPR 2021)
Wang et al., 'Towards Fairness in Visual Recognition' (CVPR 2020)
Denton et al., 'Image Counterfactual Sensitivity Analysis for Detecting Unintended Bias'

probe_designs.content: Evaluate trained model on corrupted versions of the CelebA test set: Gaussian noise, JPEG compression, motion blur, Gaussian blur, brightness, contrast, fog, elastic transform — each at 5 severities. Compute mean Corruption Error (mCE) and per-attribute robustness degradation. Also test adversarial robustness with FGSM (ε=4/255) and PGD-10. Healthy signal: graceful degradation (< 5% mean accuracy drop at mid-severity); attributes depending on global color (Pale_Skin, Gray_Hair) should be more sensitive to brightness/contrast than shape-based ones (Oval_Face) — a coherent pattern indicates the model uses plausible features. Unhealthy signal: sharp cliff-drops at low severity, or adversarial drop > 50% with ε=4/255, indicating reliance on non-robust texture features.
possible_sources: Hendrycks & Dietterich, 'Benchmarking Neural Network Robustness to Common Corruptions' (ICLR 2019)
Goodfellow et al., 'Explaining and Harnessing Adversarial Examples' (ICLR 2015)
Madry et al., 'Towards Deep Learning Models Resistant to Adversarial Attacks' (ICLR 2018)

probe_designs.probe_name: Cross-Dataset Generalization to LFWA and MAAD-Face
probe_designs.content: Evaluate the CelebA-trained model zero-shot on LFWA (Labeled Faces in the Wild with attributes; 13k images, same 40 attributes) and on a subset of MAAD-Face. Measure mean accuracy and per-attribute accuracy delta (CelebA_test → LFWA). Healthy signal: drop of 3–8% mean accuracy is expected and acceptable (domain shift from celebrity to in-the-wild); structural attributes (Male, Eyeglasses, Wearing_Hat) transfer best; appearance-centric attributes (Attractive, Young) transfer worst because labels are subjective and annotator-dependent. Unhealthy signal: drop > 15%, indicating the model over-fit CelebA-specific biases such as studio lighting, centered crops, or celebrity-typical makeup.
possible_sources: Liu et al., ICCV 2015 — CelebA and LFWA proposed jointly
Terhörst et al., 'MAAD-Face: A Massively Annotated Attribute Dataset' (IEEE TIFS 2021)
Günther et al., 'AFFACT: Alignment-Free Facial Attribute Classification'

probe_designs.content: Generate Grad-CAM / Score-CAM / Integrated-Gradients saliency maps for a stratified sample of images, separately for each of the 40 attributes. Verify that saliency concentrates on the anatomically correct region: 'Wearing_Hat' on the top of the head, 'Eyeglasses' around the eyes, 'Goatee'/'Mustache' on the lower face, 'Wearing_Necklace' on the neck. Compute pointing-game accuracy vs. facial landmarks (from dlib/MTCNN). Healthy signal: > 70% pointing-game accuracy on spatially localized attributes. Unhealthy signal: saliency drifts to irrelevant regions (e.g., background, hair pixels for 'Smiling'), or same map for all attributes — indicating shortcut learning and possibly a broken last-stage pooling or classifier.
possible_sources: Selvaraju et al., 'Grad-CAM: Visual Explanations from Deep Networks via Gradient-based Localization' (ICCV 2017)
Sundararajan et al., 'Axiomatic Attribution for Deep Networks' (ICML 2017)
Zhang et al., 'Top-down Neural Attention by Excitation Backprop' (ECCV 2016 — pointing game)

probe_designs.content: CelebA attributes like 'Attractive', 'Young', 'Oval_Face', 'Big_Lips', 'Pointy_Nose', 'Pale_Skin' are known to be subjective and noisily annotated. Identify high-loss training examples (top-1% loss per attribute) and inspect them manually / with CLIP-based similarity scoring. Train a small 'model confidence vs. label' agreement check: use cross-validated predictions, flag samples where the model disagrees with the label with high confidence — candidate label-noise set. Healthy signal: high-loss samples visibly ambiguous to humans (noise is intrinsic). Unhealthy signal: systematic label errors — for example, 'Young' flipped on specific identity clusters, or 'Blond_Hair' and 'Brown_Hair' simultaneously positive. Report estimated noise rate per attribute; attributes with noise > 15% should be flagged as upper-bounded in accuracy.
possible_sources: Northcutt et al., 'Confident Learning: Estimating Uncertainty in Dataset Labels' (JAIR 2021)
Northcutt et al., 'Pervasive Label Errors in Test Sets Destabilize ML Benchmarks' (NeurIPS 2021)
Lingenfelter et al., 'A Quantitative Analysis of Labeling Issues in the CelebA Dataset' (ISVC 2022)

probe_designs.probe_name: Backbone Capacity vs. Attribute Correlation Efficiency
probe_designs.content: Measure training efficiency signals: (a) FLOPs and parameter count of the timm backbone; (b) throughput (images/sec) at train and inference; (c) convergence speed (epochs to reach 90% mean accuracy); (d) whether a shared backbone + 40 linear heads is under-utilized by checking the rank and mutual information of the final-layer features across attributes. Also compute the 40×40 attribute co-occurrence matrix from labels vs. the 40×40 correlation matrix of predicted logits — they should be similar (Frobenius distance small). Healthy signal: predicted-logit correlation ≈ label correlation (model captures attribute structure); GPU-hours to convergence comparable to reported timm + CelebA baselines (~2–4 hours on a single A100 for ResNet-50). Unhealthy signal: logit correlations all ≈ 0 (independent heads, no representation sharing) or all ≈ 1 (degenerate collapse), indicating mis-tuned learning rate or a broken shared feature extractor.
possible_sources: Hand & Chellappa, 'Attributes for Improved Attributes: A Multi-Task Network for Attribute Classification' (AAAI 2017)
Cao et al., 'Partially Shared Multi-Task CNN for Pedestrian Attribute Recognition'
He et al., 'Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition' (CVPR 2016)
timm library benchmarks (Wightman, 2021)
